stories.headline: Meta CTO Bosworth Tells Staff the Company Did an 'Atrocious Job' Selling Its AI Vision
stories.key_takeaway: Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth admitted in an internal memo that the company did an 'atrocious job' explaining its Applied AI reorganization and undermined employee trust, promising better communication and restored perks after cutting roughly 8,000 jobs.

pull_quote.text: undermined the trust employees had in the value of their expertise and career growth

data_points.label: Engineers and PMs in Applied AI

stories.article_excerpt: Andrew Bosworth has built a reputation inside Meta as the executive willing to say the quiet part out loud. This week he did again.

stories.headline: Runware Raises $50 Million Series A to Scale Its 'Sonic' AI Inference Engine
stories.key_takeaway: Runware's $50M Series A, led by Dawn Capital, signals that AI venture money is shifting from training models to the inference layer that runs them cheaply at scale.

pull_quote.text: On the software side, we heavily optimize model loading and offloading, which lets us support over 400,000 models and make any of them available for inference in real time.

stories.article_excerpt: When Flaviu Radulescu set out to build a text-to-image company in 2023, he kept running into the same wall: the cost of inference.

stories.headline: Infosys Strikes Strategic OpenAI Collaboration to Scale Enterprise AI Transformation
stories.key_takeaway: As frontier labs concede that integration and change management, not raw model intelligence, are the true barriers to enterprise AI value, systems integrators like Infosys are becoming the indispensable channel for deploying frontier models at scale.

pull_quote.text: Our collaboration with OpenAI establishes an operating model to unlock AI value at scale, uniting technology, talent, and transformation playbooks so clients can move decisively from pilots to performance.

data_points.label: GenAI pilots that never reach production

stories.article_excerpt: Infosys pairs OpenAI's models with its Topaz fabric as systems integrators become the frontier-model enterprise channel.

stories.headline: Palantir Pushes Enterprise AI Beyond Government With New Google Cloud, Legal and Construction Deals
stories.key_takeaway: At AIPCon 10, Palantir deepened its pivot from government to embedded enterprise workflows, pairing a multi-tiered Google Cloud partnership with production AIP deployments at Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy and GNP Seguros.

pull_quote.text: By uniting BigQuery and Gemini with Palantir's Foundry and AIP, we're giving joint customers a secure, unified foundation to run their most complex, high-stakes workflows at scale.
pull_quote.role: VP, Applied AI & Platform Ecosystem, Google Cloud

stories.article_excerpt: At AIPCon 10, Palantir paired a multi-tiered Google Cloud deal with enterprise AIP deployments in law, construction and insurance.

stories.headline: Defakto Raises $30.75 Million to Secure the Identities of AI Agents
stories.key_takeaway: Defakto's $30.75M Series B signals that enterprise identity security is shifting from protecting humans to governing the machines and AI agents that now vastly outnumber them on corporate networks.

pull_quote.text: Every enterprise has invested in securing its people. But non-humans are now the majority of users, and their identities are still governed by outdated, secret-based models that create mounting technical debt.

stories.article_excerpt: Defakto raised $30.75M to secure the identities of AI agents and other non-human actors that now outnumber humans on networks.

stories.headline: Satya Nadella's 'Loopcraft' Essay Lays Out Microsoft's Case for Building Frontier AI Ecosystems
stories.key_takeaway: Nadella's viral 'Loopcraft' essay argues the AI economy will be won by companies that own a compounding learning loop of human and token capital, not by those who rent the best frontier model.

pull_quote.text: The real opportunity is not in picking the best model but instead in building a learning loop on top of models where human capital and token capital compound. You can offload a task, or even a job, but you can never offload your learning.

stories.article_excerpt: Nadella's first-ever long-form essay argues the AI economy will be won by owning the loop, not the model.

stories.headline: The Coding-Model Wars Splinter Into Camps as MiniMax, Qwen and Kimi Crowd the Open-Weight Frontier
stories.key_takeaway: The open-weight coding-model field has fragmented into competing camps while a release every two days outpaces independent benchmarking, leaving most performance claims vendor-reported and unverified.

pull_quote.text: There are no independent third-party numbers for K2.7 on the standard public suites yet, which means a true apples-to-apples comparison with Claude, GPT or DeepSeek does not exist.
pull_quote.attribution: Post-launch analysis of Kimi K2.7 Code

stories.article_excerpt: In the seventy-two hours after Moonshot pushed Kimi K2.7 Code, two more coding models landed and a leaderboard reshuffled twice.

stories.headline: Justice Department Calls xAI 'Vital' to National Security in Bid to Toss NAACP Pollution Suit
stories.key_takeaway: The DOJ asked a federal court to dismiss the NAACP's Clean Air Act suit over xAI's unpermitted gas turbines in Memphis, arguing the data centers are vital to military operations and pitting environmental-justice claims against national-security deference.

pull_quote.text: The NAACP's lawsuit threatens American national, economic, and energy security by seeking to shut off the power supply for artificial-intelligence innovation that supports the Department of War's military operations.

data_points.label: Unpermitted gas turbines at xAI Memphis
AI models the DOD says support classified ops
More turbines SpaceX plans to buy

stories.article_excerpt: The DOJ asked a court to toss the NAACP's pollution suit against xAI, calling the company's Memphis turbines vital to national security.

stories.headline: Sundar Pichai Faces Boos and a Walkout at Stanford Commencement Over Google's Defense AI Ties
stories.key_takeaway: Roughly 200 Stanford graduates walked out of Sundar Pichai's commencement address to protest Google's defense AI ties, making military and surveillance uses of AI the focus of campus dissent.

pull_quote.text: We are walking out because we refuse to glorify the corporations that fuel this violence and exercise our power to choose differently.

stories.article_excerpt: Roughly 200 Stanford graduates walked out on Sundar Pichai's commencement speech over Google's Project Nimbus and ICE ties.

stories.headline: Colorado's First-in-Nation AI Discrimination Law Faces a DOJ and xAI Challenge as Its June 30 Deadline Nears
stories.key_takeaway: Colorado's pioneering AI anti-discrimination law became the first state AI statute to draw a DOJ intervention and an xAI lawsuit, making it the leading test of federal-versus-state authority to regulate AI.

pull_quote.text: Laws like Colorado's that force AI models to produce false results or promote ideological bias threaten national and economic security and must be stopped.
pull_quote.role: Assistant Attorney General, U.S. DOJ Civil Division

stories.article_excerpt: Colorado's first-in-nation AI discrimination law drew a DOJ intervention and xAI suit, freezing its June 30 deadline.

stories.headline: QPILOTS Steers Flow and Diffusion Policies at Test Time, Sidestepping Brittle RL Fine-Tuning
stories.key_takeaway: QPILOTS improves expressive flow and diffusion robot policies by steering a frozen generative model with a learned Q-function at inference time, avoiding the instability and cost of backpropagating RL through the sampling chain.
stories.article_excerpt: A new paper argues you can improve expressive robot policies by steering them at test time, not fine-tuning them with brittle RL.

stories.headline: PhoneHarness Teaches Phone-Use Agents to Mix GUI Taps, Command Lines and Tool Calls
stories.key_takeaway: PhoneHarness argues capable phone-use agents should not be limited to imitating human taps; a mixed action space of GUI gestures, command-line commands and tool calls lets an agent choose the most reliable path to a goal.
stories.article_excerpt: A new paper argues the most capable mobile agents won't just tap screens; they'll reach past the interface to a shell or tool call.

stories.headline: Relational Structural Causal Models Push AI Toward Reasoning About Interventions, Not Just Patterns
stories.key_takeaway: Ejaz and Bareinboim extend structural causal models to relational, multi-entity settings, deriving when AI can rigorously answer questions about unseen object combinations and interventions rather than merely matching patterns.

pull_quote.text: An artificial intelligence must have a model of its environment that is causal, supporting reasoning about interventions and counterfactuals, and also combinatorial, supporting generalization to unseen combinations of objects.

stories.article_excerpt: Modern AI systems are spectacular pattern-matchers, but a new paper argues genuine causal reasoning is foundational, not optional.

stories.headline: Dr-DCI Lets Search Agents Talk Directly to a Corpus, Expanding Their Workspace on the Fly
stories.key_takeaway: Dr-DCI replaces the retriever-mediated interface with direct corpus interaction, shell-executable search and verify operations scaled via dynamic workspace expansion, giving long-horizon research agents direct, growable access to their evidence.

pull_quote.text: exposes evidence only as ranked results or bounded document views
pull_quote.role: Dr-DCI paper, on the limits of retriever-mediated interfaces

stories.article_excerpt: Most AI research agents see the world through a narrow window: a retriever's ranked list. Dr-DCI lets them touch the corpus.
